original_datasets.name: Benin 2012 Survey Experiment (Yayi spouse ethnicity prime)
original_datasets.columns: [id, passage, ethnie, BoniVote, bonivote, sexe, age, department, Cotonou, education]

summary_statistics.info: Dataset has 1104 rows and 67 columns. Key fields: passage (categorical; includes FonFemme, Femme, Control, Nago, Bariba), ethnie (categorical; includes Fon, Yorouba, others), BoniVote (0/1).

original_datasets.notes: Analysis filters to three conditions in 'passage' (FonFemme, Femme, Control) and excludes Yorouba coethnics. Both 'BoniVote' and 'bonivote' columns are present; the analysis uses 'BoniVote'.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restriction.

analysis_path.path_description: Filter to Control/Femme/FonFemme, exclude Yorouba, recode vote, construct coethnic and condition factors, then fit logistic regression with interaction vote ~ condition * coethnic.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Experimental condition with levels control, wife, wife(FON).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Exclude Yorouba coethnics; keep only Control/Femme/FonFemme conditions.
key_choices.data_processing: Factor construction for condition and coethnic; baseline setting; interaction included in model.
key_choices.model_choice: logistic regression (binomial GLM with logit link).
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value for the interaction term and model LR test.

outcome.name: vote
outcome.definition: Binary indicator of willingness to vote for President Yayi (1=yes, 0=no) from BoniVote.

main_predictor.name: condition
main_predictor.definition: Treatment condition: control (no spouse info), wife (spouse mentioned), wife(FON) (spouse mentioned as Fon).

analysis_code.notes: Python translation of the provided R script (analysisClaim.R). Reads from /app/data/Benin2012survey.csv and prints coefficients including the interaction.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Restrict analysis to Benin-related survey experiment data.

analysis_path.path_description: Same as Task1 using the Benin 2012 survey experiment subset: vote ~ condition * coethnic after filtering and exclusions.
preprocessing_steps: Construct coethnic and condition as in Task1.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Experimental condition with levels control, wife, wife(FON).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Benin 2012 survey experiment; exclude Yorouba; keep Control/Femme/FonFemme conditions.
key_choices.model_choice: logistic regression (binomial GLM with logit link).

outcome.name: vote
outcome.definition: Binary indicator of willingness to vote for President Yayi (1=yes, 0=no) from BoniVote.

analysis_code.notes: Python translation of the provided R script (analysisTask2.R). Reads from /app/data/Benin2012survey.csv and prints interaction coefficients.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned interaction logistic regression path; do not alter the dataset or focal claim.

files.data/analysisClaim__py.py: Python entry script for Task1. Loads Benin2012survey.csv from /app/data, applies filters and recodes, fits GLM logit with interaction, and prints coefficients and LR test.
files.data/analysisTask2__py.py: Python entry script for Task2. Same pipeline as Task1; prints interaction coefficients.

codebase.notes: Python rewrites replace the original R scripts to comply with execution policy. Ensure the data directory is mounted to /app/data so the scripts can read Benin2012survey.csv.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 Python scripts using the original dataset. Capture and store printed model summaries, including the interaction coefficient and p-value.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: Fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Key: coefficient and p-value for the interaction term C(condition)[T.wife(FON)]:C(coethnic)[T.co-ethnic(FON)], LR test for interaction, and conclusion text on whether the interaction supports increased support among spouse coethnics.
